informal_statement.text: Given equal-length slices of non-negative item weights and item values, and a non-negative integer capacity, return the maximum total value achievable by choosing a subset whose total weight does not exceed the capacity. Each item may be selected at most once.
preconditions: weights and values have the same length
every weight is a non-negative integer
every value is a non-negative integer
required_properties: the result is witnessed by some subset S of item indices whose total weight <= capacity and total value equals the result
no subset whose total weight <= capacity has a strictly greater total value
each item index is used at most once in any witness subset
edge_cases: capacity 0 (result is 0 unless there is a zero-weight positive-value item)
a single item with weight > capacity
multiple items of identical weight and value

semantic_units.description: Alignment precondition: weights and values have the same length.
common_failure_modes: allowing trailing values to be ignored

semantic_units.description: Capacity feasibility: the witness subset has total weight at most the capacity.
Value witness: there is a valid selection whose total value equals the returned result.
common_failure_modes: stating only a value upper bound

semantic_units.description: Optimality: no valid selection with total weight <= capacity achieves a strictly greater total value.
0/1 uniqueness: items appear at most once in any witness selection.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ws : Weights) (vs : Values) (cap : Nat), ws.length = vs.length → ∃ (sel : Selection), ValidSelection ws sel ∧ totalWeight ws sel ≤ cap ∧ totalValue vs sel = knapsack01 ws vs cap
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values are length-aligned, there exists a valid feasible selection whose total value equals the returned result.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ws : Weights) (vs : Values) (cap : Nat) (sel : Selection), ws.length = vs.length → ValidSelection ws sel → totalWeight ws sel ≤ cap → totalValue vs sel ≤ knapsack01 ws vs cap
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values are length-aligned, no valid feasible selection beats the returned result.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  (∀ x ∈ w, True) →
  (∀ x ∈ v, True) →
  True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the inputs are well-formed: weights and values are lists of natural numbers (non-negative by type), and they have equal length. Capacity is a natural number (non-negative by type).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  let n := length w;
  let items := List.range n;
  ∀ (S : Finset (Fin n)),
    (∑ i in S, w.get i) ≤ cap →
    (∑ i in S, v.get i) ≤ knap w v cap
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the knapsack optimum as the maximum total value over all subsets of indices whose total weight is within capacity. The result returned by the implementation is an upper bound on every feasible subset value.

There exists an optimal feasible subset achieving the maximum value (finiteness of subsets over a finite index set). This obligation is typically discharged by using `Finset.max'`/`Nat.find`-style reasoning in the proof.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  ∀ (i : Nat) (c : Nat),
  i ≤ length w →
  c ≤ cap →
  dp i c =
    (Finset.sup' (Finset.filter (fun S : Finset (Fin i)) (fun S => (∑ j in S, (w.get ⟨j.1, _⟩)) ≤ c))
      (fun S => ∑ j in S, (v.get ⟨j.1, _⟩)))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The dynamic-programming table entry `dp i c` computed by the implementation equals the maximum total value achievable using only the first `i` items with total weight at most `c`.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  ∀ (c : Nat), c ≤ cap →
  dp 0 c = 0
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: With zero items, the best achievable value is 0 for any capacity.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  ∀ (i c : Nat),
  i < length w →
  c ≤ cap →
  let wi := w.get ⟨i, by simpa [List.length] using ‹i < length w›⟩;
  let vi := v.get ⟨i, by simpa [List.length] using ‹i < length v›⟩;
  dp (i+1) c =
    if wi ≤ c then max (dp i c) (dp i (c - wi) + vi) else dp i c
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The DP recurrence matches the 0/1 knapsack choice: either skip item `i` (value `dp i c`) or take it (if it fits) (value `dp i (c-wi) + vi`).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  ∀ (i c : Nat),
  i ≤ length w →
  c ≤ cap →
  ∀ (S : Finset (Fin i)),
    (∑ j in S, w.get j) ≤ c →
    (∑ j in S, v.get j) ≤ dp i c
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Any feasible subset of the first `i` items with total weight ≤ `c` has total value ≤ the DP optimum `dp i c`.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  ∀ (i c : Nat),
  i ≤ length w →
  c ≤ cap →
  ∃ (S : Finset (Fin i)),
    (∑ j in S, w.get j) ≤ c ∧
    (∑ j in S, v.get j) = dp i c
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The DP value `dp i c` is achieved by some feasible subset of the first `i` items.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  knap w v cap = dp (length w) cap
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation’s returned value equals the DP entry for using all items and the full capacity.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat),
  length w = length v →
  knap w v cap =
    Finset.sup' (Finset.filter (fun S : Finset (Fin (length w)))
      (fun S => (∑ i in S, w.get i) ≤ cap))
      (fun S => ∑ i in S, v.get i)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value equals the maximum total value over all subsets of indices whose total weight does not exceed `cap`.
